tajweed.quizDesc: Measure your tajweed knowledge with a short review.

levels.beginnerDesc: Learn the core tajweed rules step by step.
levels.intermediateDesc: Move into intermediate rules that strengthen your recitation.

aiCheck.title: AI Pronunciation Check
aiCheck.desc: Analyze your recitation with AI and get instant feedback.

med_tabii.title: Natural Madd
med_tabii.rule: When a madd letter is not followed by a hamza or sukoon, it is elongated for two counts.

0.explanation: The alif acts as a natural elongation letter.

1.explanation: The waw acts as a natural elongation letter.

idgam_meal_gunne.title: Idgham with Ghunnah
idgam_meal_gunne.rule: If Sakin Noon or Tanween is followed by one of the Yemnu letters, it is merged with ghunnah.

0.explanation: The Noon meets Ya, so Idgham occurs.

1.explanation: The Noon meets Meem, so Idgham occurs.

idgam-bila-gunne.title: Idgham without Ghunnah
idgam-bila-gunne.rule: If Sakin Noon or Tanween is followed by Lam or Ra, it is merged without ghunnah.

0.explanation: The Noon meets Ra, so Idgham without ghunnah occurs.

1.explanation: The Tanween meets Lam, so Idgham without ghunnah occurs.

ihfa.title: Ikhfa
ihfa.rule: If Sakin Noon or Tanween is followed by an Ikhfa letter, the Noon sound is concealed with a nasal sound.

0.explanation: The Sakin Noon meets Za, so Ikhfa occurs.

1.explanation: The Sakin Noon meets Shin, so Ikhfa occurs.

izhar.title: Izhar
izhar.rule: If Sakin Noon or Tanween is followed by a throat letter, the Noon sound is pronounced clearly.

0.explanation: The Sakin Noon meets Kha, so Izhar occurs.

1.explanation: The Tanween meets Alif, so Izhar occurs.

iklab.title: Iqlab
iklab.rule: If Sakin Noon or Tanween is followed by Ba, the Noon sound is converted to Meem with ghunnah.

0.explanation: The Sakin Noon meets Ba, so it changes to Meem.

1.explanation: The Tanween meets Ba, so it changes to Meem.

kalkale.title: Qalqalah
kalkale.rule: When the letters of Qalqalah are sakin, they are pronounced with an echoing vibration.

0.explanation: Qaf is sakin, so Qalqalah occurs.

1.explanation: Dal is sakin, so Qalqalah occurs.

ra_hukmu.title: Rules of Ra
ra_hukmu.desc: How the letter Ra is pronounced
ra_hukmu.rule: Ra with fatha or dhamma is heavy, Ra with kasra is light. In sukoon form, the previous vowel determines the reading.

0.explanation: Ra with fatha is read heavily.

med_muttasil.title: Connected Madd
med_muttasil.rule: When a madd letter is followed by a hamza in the same word, it becomes Connected Madd and is read longer.

0.explanation: The alif and hamza are in the same word, so the sound is elongated longer.
